title: नृसिंह चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ दोहा ॥
श्री नृसिंह प्रभु चरन सरोज, मन में धरि ध्यान।
चालीसा नृहरि प्रभु, कहूँ करि प्रणाम॥
सकल विपत हरि लीजिये, प्रभु जय नरहरि राय।
भक्त प्रहलाद उबारि के, दनुज हिरण्यकशिपु मराय॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय नरसिंह भगवान हमारे।
भक्त प्रहलाद के प्राण रखवारे॥
हिरण्यकशिपु दैत्य संहारा।
खम्भ फाड़ि प्रकटे सुखकारा॥
उग्र रूप प्रभु दर्शन दीन्हा।
नखन विदारि दैत्य बध कीन्हा॥
गोद में लेकर दैत्य को मारा।
भक्त प्रहलाद किया उबारा॥
ब्रह्मा विष्णु महेश पुकारे।
नृसिंह प्रभु सबके दुख टारे॥
संध्या समय प्रकट भगवाना।
न नर न पशु अद्भुत रूप सुजाना॥
द्वार देहरी पर बैठाया।
न भीतर न बाहर प्रभु आया॥
न अस्त्र न शस्त्र से मारा।
नख से विदारि दैत्य संहारा॥
ब्रह्मा का वर सत्य बनाया।
प्रहलाद को संकट से बचाया॥
होलिका दहन किया उस काला।
भक्त प्रहलाद रहा सुखशाला॥
लक्ष्मी संग विराजे सोहे।
भक्तन के मन नित प्रभु मोहे॥
शंख चक्र गदा पद्म विराजे।
नृसिंह प्रभु के मंगल साजे॥
वेद पुराण सबन गुन गावें।
नृसिंह चरण शरण जो आवें॥
सकल भय हरण प्रभु तुम्हारे।
भक्तन के जीवन उजियारे॥
तुम्हरे भजन से सुख सरसावे।
संकट मिटे दोष सब जावे॥
जो नृसिंह को ध्यान लगावे।
सो सब विपत विनाश करावे॥
धरम करम सब सिद्ध बनावे।
संसार सागर पार करावे॥
अहोबिलम धाम तव प्यारा।
जहाँ निवास करत रखवारा॥
नौ नरसिंह रूप बखाने।
उग्र योग वीर सब जाने॥
ज्वाला मालिनी रूप सुहावन।
लक्ष्मी नृसिंह छवि मन भावन॥
प्रयाग में मूर्ति विराजे।
नरसिंह कथा सब जग साजे॥
सनक सनातन ध्यान लगावें।
नारद मुनि गुण गान सुनावें॥
देवता सब मिलि जय जय गावें।
नृसिंह प्रभु की शरण में आवें॥
भक्त जनन के पापन हारी।
कृपा करो प्रभु विश्ववारी॥
सकल मनोरथ पूरण कीजे।
भक्तन को अभय पद दीजे॥
जो यह चालीसा नित गावे।
नृसिंह कृपा नित प्रति पावे॥
संकट कटे मिटे भव पीरा।
जपत सदा जय नरहरि बीरा॥
रोग शोक सब दूर पलाये।
नृसिंह प्रभु सब कष्ट मिटाये॥
घर में सुख सम्पत्ति आवे।
नरसिंह भजन जो नित गावे॥
शत्रु विनाश करे भगवाना।
भक्त जनन को देत कल्याणा॥
विष्णु अवतारी प्रभु सोहे।
सकल चराचर जगत को मोहे॥
प्रभु नृसिंह की महिमा भारी।
कोटि ब्रह्म सम पूजन हारी॥
चालीसा पढ़ करो विनंती।
मिटे कष्ट सब होय अनंती॥
भय बिनु होय सदा सुखदाई।
नृसिंह चरण तरे यह भाई॥
जो चालीसा पढ़े सुनावे।
सो सब सुख सम्पदा पावे॥

॥ दोहा ॥
नृसिंह चालीसा पढ़े, जो कोई करे प्रणाम।
सकल मनोरथ सिद्ध हों, पावे उत्तम धाम॥
transliteration:
|| Doha ||
Shri Nrisimha Prabhu Charan Saroj, Man Mein Dhari Dhyan.
Chalisa Nrihari Prabhu, Kahun Kari Pranam.
Sakal Vipat Hari Lijiye, Prabhu Jai Narhari Rai.
Bhakt Prahlad Ubaari Ke, Danuj Hiranyakashipu Marai.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Narsimha Bhagwan Hamare.
Bhakt Prahlad Ke Pran Rakhvare.
Hiranyakashipu Daitya Sanhara.
Khambh Phadi Prakte Sukhkara.
Ugra Roop Prabhu Darshan Dinha.
Nakhan Vidari Daitya Badh Kinha.
God Mein Lekar Daitya Ko Mara.
Bhakt Prahlad Kiya Ubara.
Brahma Vishnu Mahesh Pukare.
Nrisimha Prabhu Sabke Dukh Tare.
Sandhya Samay Prakat Bhagwana.
Na Nar Na Pashu Adbhut Roop Sujana.
Dwar Dehri Par Baithaya.
Na Bhitar Na Bahar Prabhu Aaya.
Na Astra Na Shastra Se Mara.
Nakh Se Vidari Daitya Sanhara.
Brahma Ka Var Satya Banaya.
Prahlad Ko Sankat Se Bachaya.
Holika Dahan Kiya Us Kala.
Bhakt Prahlad Raha Sukhshala.
Lakshmi Sang Viraje Sohe.
Bhaktan Ke Man Nit Prabhu Mohe.
Shankh Chakra Gada Padma Viraje.
Nrisimha Prabhu Ke Mangal Saje.
Ved Puran Saban Gun Gave.
Nrisimha Charan Sharan Jo Ave.
Sakal Bhay Haran Prabhu Tumhare.
Bhaktan Ke Jivan Ujiyare.
Tumhre Bhajan Se Sukh Sarsave.
Sankat Mite Dosh Sab Jave.
Jo Nrisimha Ko Dhyan Lagave.
So Sab Vipat Vinash Karave.
Dharam Karam Sab Siddh Banave.
Sansar Sagar Par Karave.
Ahobilam Dham Tav Pyara.
Jahan Nivas Karat Rakhwara.
Nau Narsimha Roop Bakhane.
Ugra Yog Veer Sab Jane.
Jwala Malini Roop Suhavan.
Lakshmi Nrisimha Chhavi Man Bhavan.
Prayag Mein Murti Viraje.
Narsimha Katha Sab Jag Saje.
Sanak Sanatan Dhyan Lagave.
Narad Muni Gun Gaan Sunave.
Devta Sab Mili Jai Jai Gave.
Nrisimha Prabhu Ki Sharan Mein Ave.
Bhakt Janan Ke Papan Hari.
Kripa Karo Prabhu Vishwavari.
Sakal Manorath Puran Kije.
Bhaktan Ko Abhay Pad Dije.
Jo Yah Chalisa Nit Gave.
Nrisimha Kripa Nit Prati Pave.
Sankat Kate Mite Bhav Peera.
Japat Sada Jai Narhari Beera.
Rog Shok Sab Door Palaye.
Nrisimha Prabhu Sab Kasht Mitaye.
Ghar Mein Sukh Sampatti Ave.
Narsimha Bhajan Jo Nit Gave.
Shatru Vinash Kare Bhagwana.
Bhakt Janan Ko Det Kalyana.
Vishnu Avatari Prabhu Sohe.
Sakal Charachar Jagat Ko Mohe.
Prabhu Nrisimha Ki Mahima Bhari.
Koti Brahm Sam Pujan Hari.
Chalisa Padh Karo Vinanti.
Mite Kasht Sab Hoy Ananti.
Bhay Binu Hoy Sada Sukhdai.
Nrisimha Charan Tare Yah Bhai.
Jo Chalisa Padhe Sunave.
So Sab Sukh Sampada Pave.

|| Doha ||
Nrisimha Chalisa Padhe, Jo Koi Kare Pranam.
Sakal Manorath Siddh Hon, Pave Uttam Dham.
hindiMeaning:
श्री नृसिंह प्रभु के चरण कमलों में ध्यान धरकर, मैं नृहरि प्रभु की चालीसा कहता हूँ। भक्त प्रहलाद को बचाकर और दैत्य हिरण्यकशिपु का वध करके जिन्होंने जगत की रक्षा की।

हे भगवान नरसिंह! आपकी जय हो। आप भक्त प्रहलाद के प्राणों के रक्षक हैं। हिरण्यकशिपु दैत्य का संहार करके खम्भ फाड़कर प्रकट हुए। उग्र रूप धरकर अपने नखों से दैत्य का वध किया। गोद में लेकर दैत्य को मारा और भक्त प्रहलाद की रक्षा की।
benefits: Provides fierce protection from enemies and evil forces
Traditionally recited for removal of deep-seated fears and phobias
Invokes courage and fearlessness in difficult times
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles caused by powerful adversaries
Invokes Lord Vishnu's most protective avatar
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. The Narsimha Chalisa, known in Sanskrit as नृसिंह चालीसा, is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Narasimha. Drawn from Traditional Chalisa Sangrah, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to provide fierce protection from enemies and evil forces and invoke Lord Vishnu's most protective avatar. It is also said to destroy obstacles caused by powerful adversaries. It is especially recommended on Saturday (Shanivar) and Sunday (Ravivar) and during Narasimha Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. The text contains 42 verses and takes around 15 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Narsimha Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Narsimha Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Powerful prayer to Lord Narasimha, the fierce avatar of Vishnu.

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure of the Narasimha Chalisa beyond the 40 chaupais?
seoFaqs.answer: Like most Hindi devotional Chalisas, the Narasimha Chalisa is structured in three parts: opening doha (couplet) verses that invoke the deity and request protection, the central body of forty chaupai verses that narrate the avatar's appearance, the killing of Hiranyakashipu, the saving of Prahlada and the Lord's various exploits, and concluding doha verses that form the phalashruti and request specific blessings. The total structural verse count is therefore 43 to 44 lines, with chalisa referring specifically to the 40-verse chaupai core. The chaupai metre, four padas of sixteen matras each, is identical to that of Tulsidas's Hanuman Chalisa, making the rhythm familiar to most Hindi-speaking devotees. (Sources: drikpanchang.com Narasimha Chalisa; vishnusahasranamam.org; vishnuchalisa.in.)

seoFaqs.question: How does the Narasimha story narrated in the Chalisa map to Bhagavata Purana 7?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chalisa compresses Bhagavata Purana Skanda 7 chapters 2 to 10, which contain the most detailed canonical narrative of Narasimha's appearance. Key episodes referenced in the chaupais include Hiranyakashipu's tapasya and Brahma's boon (chapters 3 to 4), the persecution of the boy-devotee Prahlada (chapters 5 to 7), the demonstration of the all-pervasive presence of Vishnu in the famous pillar episode (chapter 8), the Lord's emergence at twilight from the cracked pillar in the half-man half-lion form, the disembowelling of Hiranyakashipu on the threshold of the palace, and Prahlada's stuti to the wrathful Lord (chapter 9). The Chalisa's compact verse-form makes it accessible to devotees who cannot read the full Sanskrit Bhagavata. (Sources: drikpanchang.com; vishnuchalisa.in; iskconkurukshetra.org.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the principal benefits the Chalisa associates with daily recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti dohas at the end of the Chalisa enumerate specific phalas: removal of bhaya (fear) of every kind including fear of enemies, fear of disease, fear of unnatural death and fear of evil spirits; protection of children, particularly small children whose vulnerability echoes Prahlada's; victory over those who obstruct dharmic action; courage to face injustice and stand up for truth; and ultimately the supreme protection of the devotee against any abhichara or black-magic attack. Narasimha is invoked specifically because his form is held to be the most ferocious protective form Vishnu ever assumed, capable of overriding even Brahma's boons granted to demonic forces. The Chalisa is therefore favoured by parents, soldiers and those facing legal or political persecution. (Sources: drikpanchang.com; vishnusahasranamam.org; iskconkurukshetra.org.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the most powerful day to begin Narasimha Chalisa parayana?
seoFaqs.answer: Narasimha Jayanti, observed on Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi (April or May depending on the year), is the supreme day for beginning a parayana. The Lord is held to have manifested at sandhya, the precise moment between day and night, on this tithi, so the recitation is traditionally timed for the pradosha hour. Other potent days include Swati nakshatra of any month (his janma-nakshatra), every Saturday (a day associated with protection from negative planetary forces), and any Chaturdashi in the lunar calendar. A 14-day Chaturdashi-to-Chaturdashi cycle, a 41-day mandala beginning at Narasimha Jayanti, and a 48-day cycle ending at the next Chaturdashi are the three commonly observed parayana lengths. (Sources: guptvrindavandham.org; iskconkurukshetra.org; hindupad.com; hindutone.com.)

seoFaqs.question: Where does Ahobilam fit in the geography of Narasimha worship that the Chalisa invokes?
seoFaqs.answer: Ahobilam in the Nallamala Hills of Andhra Pradesh is held to be the very site of the avatar's appearance and is the principal Narasimha kshetra in India. The complex consists of nine separate shrines, the Nava Narasimha, each enshrining a distinct mood and form of the Lord: Ahobila Narasimha (the central wrathful form), Varaha Narasimha, Malola Narasimha (with Lakshmi), Jwala Narasimha (the form just after killing Hiranyakashipu), Yogananda, Chatravata, Karanja, Pavana and Bhargava. The Chalisa, while not naming Ahobilam directly, draws on the iconography crystallised at this kshetra, particularly the Jwala and Ugra Narasimha forms. Pilgrims commonly recite the Chalisa during the difficult trek between the nine shrines. (Sources: Wikipedia Ahobilam; pureprayer.com; iskconkurukshetra.org.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Narasimha Chalisa differ from the Narasimha Kavach of Prahlada?
seoFaqs.answer: The two texts share the deity but differ in language, source and ritual function. Narasimha Chalisa is a Hindi devotional composition of forty chaupais following the Tulsidas-style chalisa template, accessible to lay devotees and used as a daily bhakti recitation. The Narasimha Kavach is a Sanskrit Tantric protection text drawn from the Brahmanda Purana and attributed to the boy-devotee Prahlada himself, and it works through anga-nyasa, assigning Narasimha-forms to specific limbs. Many devotees recite both: the Kavach in the morning as a protective shield before leaving home, and the Chalisa at sandhya or before sleep as a bhakti-praise. The Kavach is shorter and denser, the Chalisa narrative and accessible. (Sources: vishnuchalisa.in; vishnusahasranamam.org; iskconkurukshetra.org.)
